architecture_summary: Admit one bounded read-only MCOI route for the SNet operator projection while keeping SNet as a Foundation Mode proof module. Do not wire SNet into gateway publication, connectors, filesystem effects, external adapters, raw answer submission, mutation routes, or autonomous execution until a future design decision, security review, rollback plan, and live evidence receipt explicitly authorize that boundary.
data_model_changes: no runtime state migration is authorized by this design
SNet mesh records remain local in-memory proof records
SNet operator output remains a bounded read-only projection with no raw answer or raw metadata exposure
SNet mesh receipts remain non-terminal evidence and cannot grant execution, connector, route, or filesystem authority
SNet route exposure is limited to a deterministic read-only operator projection and does not accept raw answers
rollback_plan: Remove mcoi/mcoi_runtime/app/routers/snet.py, remove the SNet router include from mcoi/mcoi_runtime/app/server_http.py, remove mcoi/tests/test_snet_router.py, restore this design gate to local-only route-blocked status, then rerun SDLC artifact validation, focused SNet tests, SNet receipt validation, guard-chain coverage, and workspace governance preflight.
migration_plan: No migration is required because this design adds only a deterministic read-only route over an in-memory seed-dependency proof mesh and does not create runtime state, external effects, public gateway endpoints, tenant records, or authority grants.
